content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 21, 1989


Mr. Lance H. Olson
Olson, Connelly, Hagel & Pong
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-597

Dear Mr. Olson:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

QUESTION

May the Jack O'Connell for Assembly Committee reimburse
another legislator's campaign committee for Assemblyman
O'Connell's proportionate share of rental and use of a county fair
booth?

CONCLUSION

The Jack O'Connell for Assembly Committee may not reimburse
another legislator's campaign committee for Mr. O'Connell's share
of the fees associated with rental and use of a fair booth.

FACTS

Mr. O'Connell proposes to share a booth with another
legislator at a county fair. The fair is within the boundaries of
the two legislators' districts. For convenience, and in order to
accommodate the accounting and booking requirements of the county
fair, the other legislator's committee proposes to pay for the
entire rental cost of the booth directly to the county fair.
Within 30 days of the event, the Jack O'Connell for Assembly
Committee proposes to reimburse the other legislator for Mr.
O'Connell's share of the fees for use of the booth.

_________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O.B. 207 • Sacramento, CA [illegible]

Mr. Lance H. Olson
Page 2


ANALYSIS

     Officeholders, candidates and their controlled committees may
not transfer campaign funds to other candidates or elected
officials. Section 85304 states:

     No candidate for elective office or committee
     controlled by that candidate or candidates
     for elective office shall transfer any contri-
     bution to any other candidate for elective
     office.

     Therefore, each legislator must pay the county fair directly for
one-half the cost of renting the booth.

     If you have additional questions concerning this matter,
please contact me at 916/322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: Mary Ann Kvadsager
                                            Political Reform Consultant

Law Offices of

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG                FPPC

                                             Oct 18 5 44 AM '89

September 25, 1989


Jeanne Pritchard
Chief Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

                RE:  Request for Advice; Jack O'Connell

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

     This office represents Assemblyman Jack O'Connell
and his controlled recipient committee Jack O'Connell
for Assembly.  On behalf of Mr. O'Connell and his
committee, we are seeking advice pursuant to the
campaign reporting provisions of the Political Reform
Act.

     Specifically, Mr. O'Connell proposes to share a
booth with another legislator at a County Fair within
the boundaries of the two legislator's districts.  As a
matter of convenience, and in order to accommodate the
accounting and booking requirements of the fair, one of
the two legislators proposes to pay for the entire
rental cost of the booth directly to the county fair
from his campaign funds.  The other legislator proposes
within 30 days to reimburse the first legislator for
his share of the fees associated with rental and use of
the fair booth from his campaign account.

     May Mr. O'Connell's campaign committee reimburse
the other legislator's campaign committee for Mr.
O'Connell's proportionate share of rental and use of
the fair booth.

     Thank you for your prompt attention in this
matter.

                                   Very truly yours,

                                   OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG


                                   LANCE. H. OLSON

                                   LHO/jph
                                   cc:  Jack O'Connell

300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, California 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 442-2952

LANCE H. OLSON
BRUCE J. HAGEL
LEROY Y FONG
ROBERT E. LEIDICH


OF COUNSEL
LLOYD G. CONNELLY, Member
   California State Legislature

lx

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 20, 1989


Lance H. Olson
Olson, Connelly, Hagel & Fong
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

                                          Re:  Letter No. 89-597

Dear Mr. Olson:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on October 18, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                          Very truly yours,

                                          [signature]
                                          Jeanne Pritchard
                                          Chief Technical Assistance
                                          and Analysis Division
